name: Boucle du Canal de Garonne et de la Garonne
name_original: Boucle du canal des deux mers
description: This 42.1-mile (67.8 km) moderate road cycling loop along the Canal des Deux Mers offers scenic views and historic towns.
region_intro: Road cycling around the Garonne-Kanal is characterized by its predominantly flat terrain, making it accessible for various skill levels. The region features extensive traffic-free paths, often converted towpaths, alongside quiet country roads. The landscape includes pastoral countryside, vineyards, and fruit-growing areas, with the Garonne River often running parallel to the cycling paths. Best road cycling routes around the Garonne-Kanal The most popular road cycling route is Bordeaux to Lacanau-Océan Greenway, a 79.3 miles (127.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 56 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows a greenway, offering long stretches of traffic-free cycling. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Loop of the Garonne, a moderate 34.4 miles (55.4 km) path. This route runs alongside the Garonne River, providing scenic views and tree-lined sections. Local road cyclists also love the Escape between vineyards and full nature, a 33.7 miles (54.3 km) trail leading through pastoral countryside and vineyards, often completed in about 2 hours 18 minutes. Road cycling around the Garonne-Kanal is defined by flat, tree-lined paths, extensive vineyards, and views of the Garonne River. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging routes with moderate elevation. The routes in Garonne canal are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 1,800 reviews. More than 40,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Garonne canal's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around the Garonne Canal?
A: There are over 2,400 road cycling routes available around the Garonne Canal. These routes cater to various skill levels, with approximately 500 easy, over 1,600 moderate, and nearly 300 difficult options, ensuring a suitable ride for everyone.

Q: What is the typical terrain like for road cycling along the Garonne Canal?
A: The Garonne Canal is renowned for its predominantly flat terrain, making it ideal for road cycling. You'll find extensive traffic-free paths, often converted towpaths, alongside quiet country roads. The landscape features pastoral countryside, vineyards, and fruit-growing areas, with the Garonne River frequently running parallel to the cycling paths.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes along the Garonne Canal?
A: Yes, the Garonne Canal is often described as a 'cyclist's dream' for families due to its mostly flat and traffic-free paths. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e826590624/bordeaux-to-lacanau-ocean-greenway" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bordeaux to Lacanau-Océan Greenway&lt;/a&gt; offer long stretches of greenway cycling, perfect for a relaxed family outing.

Q: What kind of natural features and landscapes can I expect to see while cycling?
A: As you cycle along the Garonne Canal, you'll experience diverse and picturesque landscapes. Expect to pass through peaceful pastoral countryside, extensive vineyards, especially near Bordeaux, and bountiful fruit-growing regions around Agen. The path often runs alongside the Garonne River, offering scenic views, and the canal banks are famously lined with ancient plane trees, creating beautiful shaded tunnels.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions along the Garonne Canal cycling routes?
A: The Garonne Canal is rich with historical and engineering marvels. Highlights include the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/929964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bordeaux Water Mirror&lt;/a&gt;, the dramatic Agen Aqueduct carrying the canal across the Garonne River, and the Cacor canal-bridge near Moissac. You can also explore charming towns like Moissac, home to UNESCO World Heritage sites, and Auvillar, regarded as one of France's most beautiful villages.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Garonne River?
A: Many routes along the Garonne Canal offer scenic views of the Garonne River. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e826606202/loop-of-the-garonne" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop of the Garonne&lt;/a&gt; runs directly alongside the river, providing picturesque vistas and tree-lined sections. You'll frequently find the canal path paralleling the river, especially in early summer when the banks are adorned with vibrant yellow irises.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling around the Garonne Canal?
A: The Garonne Canal is enjoyable for road cycling throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery, with fewer crowds. Summer is also popular, especially with the shade provided by the tree-lined paths, offering respite from the sun. The region's generally mild climate makes it accessible for cycling for extended periods.

Q: Can I find routes that explore the local vineyards?
A: Absolutely. The region around the Garonne Canal is known for its extensive vineyards. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e825348799/escape-between-vineyards-and-full-nature" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Escape between vineyards and full nature&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e831788571/escape-between-the-garonne-and-the-vineyards" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Escape between the Garonne and the vineyards&lt;/a&gt; specifically lead through pastoral countryside and vineyards, allowing you to immerse yourself in the wine-growing landscape.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Garonne Canal?
A: The Garonne Canal area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 1,800 reviews. Cyclists often praise the flat, traffic-free paths, the serene ambiance created by the tree-lined canal banks, and the blend of natural beauty with historical towns and engineering marvels.

Q: Are there any longer road cycling routes for more advanced riders?
A: While many routes are flat, there are options for longer distances and some with moderate elevation. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e826590624/bordeaux-to-lacanau-ocean-greenway" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bordeaux to Lacanau-Océan Greenway&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 79.3-mile (127.6 km) route. For those seeking more challenging climbs, some routes venture towards the Pyrenees foothills, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e943692251/between-bagneres-de-luchon-and-baussens" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Between Bagnères de Luchon and Baussens&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant elevation gain.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in the Garonne Canal region?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available for road cyclists. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e826606202/loop-of-the-garonne" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop of the Garonne&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular path that allows you to start and end in the same location while enjoying scenic river views. Other loops like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e826344696/roger-lapebie-trail-and-la-sauve-majeure-abbey-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roger Lapébie Trail and La Sauve-Majeure Abbey Loop&lt;/a&gt; also offer excellent circular options.

Q: What kind of food and drink can I expect to find along the routes?
A: The Garonne Canal region is renowned for its gastronomy. You'll have opportunities to sample local cuisine and products, including the famous Prunes Agenais, especially around Agen. Many towns and villages along the canal, such as Moissac and Valence d'Agen, offer cafes, restaurants, and local markets where you can enjoy regional specialties and refuel during your ride.
tips:
This Canal is part of the route better known under the name of "Canal des deux Mers" which connects Bordeaux to Sète. In its oceanic part, from Bordeaux to the Seuil de Norouze after Toulouse, it takes the track "La Roger Lapébie" then the Canal Lateral à la Garonne. In its southern part, it becomes the "Canal du Midi"
If you travel this route in its entirety, prefer a mountain bike or gravel bike to the road bike whose tires that are too thin will give you too much discomfort and are unsuitable for certain passages.
Very nice view of the medieval city

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling loop?
faqs.answer: This loop primarily follows the Canal Latéral à la Garonne, which is known for its predominantly flat and generally straight terrain. While much of the larger Canal des Deux Mers route features dedicated cycle lanes with smooth surfaces, some sections can be unpaved or have a thin layer of gravel. Given this is a racebike tour, expect mostly smooth paths, but be prepared for occasional rougher patches. The overall elevation gain is minimal.

faqs.question: Is this cycling loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this loop is generally considered easy and leisurely, making it suitable for a wide range of cyclists, including beginners. The route's primarily flat nature keeps the physical demands low. However, as it's designated for racebikes, ensure your bike is suitable for potentially varied surfaces, including some unpaved sections mentioned in the broader Canal des Deux Mers context.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: Along this loop, you'll ride alongside the historic The Canal Latéral à la Garonne, enjoying picturesque landscapes of vineyards and wheat fields. You'll also encounter charming historic towns and have the opportunity to take in the View of Saint-Macaire. The route offers diverse scenery, from shaded paths under plane trees to views of slow-moving pleasure boats.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this loop?
faqs.answer: The Canal des Deux Mers region, which this loop is part of, is generally pleasant for cycling from spring through early autumn. Spring (April-May) and early autumn (September-October) offer comfortable temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be warm, so starting early in the day is advisable if cycling during July or August.

faqs.question: Are there any specific parking options or trailhead access points for this loop?
faqs.answer: While specific parking for this exact loop isn't detailed, the Canal des Deux Mers route is well-serviced. You can typically find parking in the towns and villages along the canal, such as Saint-Macaire or other access points to the Canal Latéral à la Garonne. Look for public parking areas in these towns to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Is this cycling loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The Canal des Deux Mers paths are generally accessible for dogs, but they should always be kept on a leash, especially given the presence of other cyclists, pedestrians, and sometimes boats. Be mindful of the terrain, as some sections might be rough for paws, and ensure you carry enough water for your dog, particularly on warmer days.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay any entrance fees to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to cycle along the Canal Latéral à la Garonne or the broader Canal des Deux Mers route. It is a public path accessible to everyone.

faqs.question: Does this loop connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this loop is part of a larger network. It intersects with sections of official trails such as EV3 France 42, Langon - Buzet-sur-Baïse, and the Canal de la Garonne - Itinéraire bis. You might also find yourself on parts of the Véloroute des Deux Mers, which is the overarching route connecting the Atlantic to the Mediterranean.

faqs.question: Are there amenities like cafes or accommodation along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Canal des Deux Mers route, including sections like this loop, is well-equipped with amenities. You'll find numerous "Accueil Vélo" (Cyclists Welcome) accommodations and services, including cafes, restaurants, and places to stay, often located within 5 km of the route. Towns like Saint-Macaire will offer options for refreshments.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a racebike tour on this loop?
faqs.answer: Given it's a racebike tour, pack light but be prepared. Essential items include a repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), water bottles, snacks, sun protection (sunscreen, sunglasses), and appropriate cycling attire. A phone for navigation and emergencies is also recommended. Consider a small backpack for personal items.

faqs.question: Can I access this loop using public transport with my bike?
faqs.answer: Yes, the broader Canal des Deux Mers route benefits from train lines running along its length, which often allow for convenient bike transport. This makes it possible to access various points of the canal system, including areas near this loop, by train. Check local train operator policies for bike carriage before you travel.

waypoints.name: The Canal Latéral à la Garonne
